title: cedrela calantas
intro: Cedrela calantas, also known as the Philippine cedar or kalantas, is a tropical tree native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of cedrelene and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Cedrela calantas include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a decoction or infusion, where the leaves or bark are steeped in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
